verdict: 2026-07-10 09:23:02 PDT — Mostly sound. The public docs, README, operator-decryptable disclosure, Simple-default/non-host lock copy, multiplier removal, endpoint list, dates, and two-backlog top-level roles now line up with the code. I found one remaining real inconsistency in the linked tactical backlog, which now functions as part of the docs source of truth.

findings.title: Tactical backlog still carries obsolete product-state claims
findings.claim: The two-backlog shape now says `reviews/backlog.md` is the detailed product/strategy backlog, but live entries in that file still say deauth is gated behind an overlay live-test matrix “not yet run,” that `rateTable`/`defaultRate`/`multiplier` persist to `localStorage`, and that browser-only storage preserves “rates/names never leave the browser.” Those claims contradict the roadmap’s matrix-dropped decision, the removed multiplier, and the server-backed/operator-decryptable store in code.
findings.alternative: Refresh the affected backlog entries in place: mark the overlay matrix as dropped, state that current config persists server-side encrypted-at-rest but operator-decryptable, remove the `multiplier`/`localStorage` current-state claims, and recast harvest/privacy notes around server-stored third-party names.
findings.win: Keeps the two-backlog design without moving items, while preventing future work from being framed from obsolete persistence, privacy, or multiplier assumptions.
